what: Specify a single hazard phase — hzr_phase
title: Specify a single hazard phase — hzr_phase
text: Creates hzr_phase object describing one term multiphase additive cumulative hazard model.  Pass list phases argument hazard() dist = "multiphase".

title: Specify a single hazard phase — hzr_phase
code: hzr_phase(   type = c("cdf", "hazard", "constant", "g3"),   t_half = 1,   nu = 1,   m = 0,   tau = 1,   gamma = 1,   alpha = 1,   eta = 1,   formula = NULL,   fixed = character(0) )

title: Specify a single hazard phase — hzr_phase
text: type Character; phase type: "cdf", "hazard", "g3", "constant". t_half Positive scalar; initial half-life (time \(G(t_{1/2}) = 0.5\)).  Used "cdf" "hazard" phases. SAS early: THALF/RHO. nu Numeric scalar; initial time exponent.  Used "cdf" "hazard" phases.  SAS early: NU. m Numeric scalar; initial shape exponent.  Used "cdf" "hazard" phases.  SAS early: M. tau Positive scalar; scale parameter "g3" phases. SAS late: TAU. gamma Positive scalar; time exponent "g3" phases. SAS late: GAMMA. alpha Non-negative scalar; shape parameter "g3" phases. alpha > 0, generic G3 formula used; alpha = 0 gives exponential limiting case.  SAS late: ALPHA. eta Positive scalar; outer exponent "g3" phases. SAS late: ETA. formula Optional one-sided formula (e.g. ~ age + nyha) phase-specific covariates.  NULL (default), phase inherits global formula hazard(). fixed Character vector naming shape parameters hold fixed optimization.  Valid names "cdf"/"hazard": "t_half", "nu", "m", "shapes" (shorthand three).  Valid names "g3": "tau", "gamma", "alpha", "eta", "shapes" (shorthand four).  Fixed parameters held starting values; mu (covariates) estimated.  Ignored "constant" phases. mirrors SAS/C HAZARD workflow shapes typically fixed scale parameters estimated.

title: Specify a single hazard phase — hzr_phase
text: S3 object class "hzr_phase" elements: type Phase type string. t_half Initial half-life (cdf/hazard phases). nu Initial time exponent (cdf/hazard phases). m Initial shape exponent (cdf/hazard phases). tau Scale parameter (g3 phases). gamma Time exponent (g3 phases). alpha Shape parameter (g3 phases). eta Outer exponent (g3 phases). formula Phase-specific formula NULL. fixed Character vector fixed parameter names (may empty).

title: Specify a single hazard phase — hzr_phase
text: "cdf" Early risk resolves time. \(\Phi(t) = G(t)\), bounded \([0, 1]\). SAS equivalent: Early / G1 phase. "hazard" Late aging risk accumulates. \(\Phi(t) = -\log(1 - G(t))\), monotone increasing. SAS equivalent: Late / G3 phase. "constant" Flat background hazard rate. \(\Phi(t) = t\). shape parameters estimated. SAS equivalent: Constant / G2 phase.

title: Specify a single hazard phase — hzr_phase
code: # Classic 3-phase Blackstone pattern early <- hzr_phase("cdf",      t_half = 0.5, nu = 2, m = 0) const <- hzr_phase("constant") late  <- hzr_phase("g3", tau = 1, gamma = 3, alpha = 1, eta = 1)  # Fix all shapes (C/SAS-style: only estimate mu) early_fixed <- hzr_phase("cdf", t_half = 0.5, nu = 2, m = 0,                           fixed = "shapes") late_fixed  <- hzr_phase("g3", tau = 1, gamma = 3, alpha = 1, eta = 1,                           fixed = "shapes")  # Fix only some parameters early_partial <- hzr_phase("cdf", t_half = 0.5, nu = 2, m = 0,                             fixed = c("nu", "m"))  # Phase with specific covariates early_cov <- hzr_phase("cdf", t_half = 0.5, nu = 2, m = 0,                         formula = ~ age + shock)  # Use in hazard(): # hazard(Surv(time, status) ~ age, data = dat, #        dist = "multiphase", #        phases = list(early = early, constant = const, late = late))

what: Cumulative hazard contribution from a single phase — hzr_phase_cumhaz
title: Cumulative hazard contribution from a single phase — hzr_phase_cumhaz
text: Computes \(\Phi_j(t)\) one phase additive model \(H(t|x) = \sum_j \mu_j(x) \Phi_j(t)\).

title: Cumulative hazard contribution from a single phase — hzr_phase_cumhaz
code: hzr_phase_cumhaz(   time,   t_half = 1,   nu = 1,   m = 0,   type = c("cdf", "hazard", "constant") )

title: Cumulative hazard contribution from a single phase — hzr_phase_cumhaz
text: time Numeric vector times (> 0). t_half Half-life parameter (> 0). nu Time exponent. m Shape parameter. type Phase type: "cdf" (early — uses \(G(t)\)), "hazard" (late — uses cumulative hazard \(h(t)\)), "constant" (flat rate — \(\Phi = t\)).

title: Cumulative hazard contribution from a single phase — hzr_phase_cumhaz
text: Numeric vector cumulative hazard contributions \(\Phi(t)\), length time.

title: Cumulative hazard contribution from a single phase — hzr_phase_cumhaz
text: "cdf": \(\Phi(t) = G(t)\).  Bounded \([0, 1]\).  Models early risk resolves time. "hazard": \(\Phi(t) = -\log(1 - G(t))\).  Monotone increasing. Models late aging risk.  cumulative hazard derived hazard function \(h(t)\), since \(\int_0^t h(s)\,ds = -\log(1 - G(t))\). "constant": \(\Phi(t) = t\).  Ignores t_half, nu, m. Equivalent exponential (constant hazard rate).

title: Cumulative hazard contribution from a single phase — hzr_phase_cumhaz
code: t_grid <- seq(0.1, 10, by = 0.1) phi_early <- hzr_phase_cumhaz(t_grid, t_half = 2, nu = 2, m = 0,                                type = "cdf") phi_late  <- hzr_phase_cumhaz(t_grid, t_half = 5, nu = 1, m = 0,                                type = "hazard") phi_const <- hzr_phase_cumhaz(t_grid, type = "constant")

what: Instantaneous hazard contribution from a single phase — hzr_phase_hazard
title: Instantaneous hazard contribution from a single phase — hzr_phase_hazard
text: Computes \(\phi_j(t) = d\Phi_j/dt\) one phase — derivative cumulative hazard contribution returned hzr_phase_cumhaz().

title: Instantaneous hazard contribution from a single phase — hzr_phase_hazard
code: hzr_phase_hazard(   time,   t_half = 1,   nu = 1,   m = 0,   type = c("cdf", "hazard", "constant") )

title: Instantaneous hazard contribution from a single phase — hzr_phase_hazard
text: time Numeric vector times (> 0). t_half Half-life parameter (> 0). nu Time exponent. m Shape parameter. type Phase type: "cdf" (early — uses \(G(t)\)), "hazard" (late — uses cumulative hazard \(h(t)\)), "constant" (flat rate — \(\Phi = t\)).

title: Instantaneous hazard contribution from a single phase — hzr_phase_hazard
text: Numeric vector instantaneous hazard contributions \(\phi(t)\), length time.

title: Instantaneous hazard contribution from a single phase — hzr_phase_hazard
text: "cdf": \(\phi(t) = g(t)\) (density). "hazard": \(\phi(t) = h(t) = g(t)/(1-G(t))\). "constant": \(\phi(t) = 1\).

title: Instantaneous hazard contribution from a single phase — hzr_phase_hazard
code: t_grid <- seq(0.1, 10, by = 0.1) phi_early <- hzr_phase_hazard(t_grid, t_half = 2, nu = 2, m = 0,                                type = "cdf") phi_late  <- hzr_phase_hazard(t_grid, t_half = 5, nu = 1, m = 0,                                type = "hazard")

title: Predict from a hazard model object — predict.hazard
text: Produces prediction outputs hazard object. Supports multiple prediction types including linear predictor, hazard, survival probability, cumulative hazard.

title: Predict from a hazard model object — predict.hazard
code: # S3 method for class 'hazard' predict(   object,   newdata = NULL,   type = c("hazard", "linear_predictor", "survival", "cumulative_hazard"),   decompose = FALSE,   ... )

title: Predict from a hazard model object — predict.hazard
text: object hazard object. newdata Optional matrix data frame predictors. types requiring time (e.g., "survival", "cumulative_hazard"), newdata include time column, time taken fitted object's data. type Prediction type: "linear_predictor": Linear predictor η = x·β (available multiphase) "hazard": Hazard scale exp(η) (available multiphase) "survival": Survival probability S(t|x) = exp(-H(t|x)) "cumulative_hazard": Cumulative hazard H(t|x) event times decompose Logical; TRUE model multiphase, return data frame per-phase cumulative hazard contributions alongside total.  Ignored single-distribution models.  Default FALSE. ... Unused; included S3 compatibility.

title: Predict from a hazard model object — predict.hazard
text: Numeric vector predictions.

title: Predict from a hazard model object — predict.hazard
text: Weibull models survival cumulative_hazard predictions: Cumulative hazard: H(t|x) = (μ·t)^ν · exp(η) Survival: S(t|x) = exp(-H(t|x)) Time values must positive finite. newdata contains time column, used; otherwise, time vector fitted object used. models fit time_windows, predictions type = "linear_predictor" "hazard" also require time values (via newdata$time fitted-time fallback) window-specific coefficients can selected.

title: Predict from a hazard model object — predict.hazard
code: # ── Basic predictions ──────────────────────────────────────────────── set.seed(1) fit <- hazard(time = rexp(50, 0.3), status = rep(1L, 50),               theta = c(0.3, 1.0), dist = "weibull", fit = TRUE) predict(fit, type = "survival") #>  [1] 0.508448836 0.315117497 0.909632142 0.913801355 0.704022509 0.034429522 #>  [7] 0.297904379 0.635876619 0.407732732 0.908685617 0.245861859 0.504733067 #> [13] 0.295096862 0.003729697 0.364948062 0.373066533 0.134498594 0.565324696 #> [19] 0.772692686 0.605268524 0.070992893 0.572924045 0.803140735 0.619329758 #> [25] 0.937239852 0.968075472 0.611315229 0.007471798 0.318195648 0.389681823 #> [31] 0.232969073 0.981596565 0.781843506 0.267479469 0.868327070 0.378412625 #> [37] 0.797695039 0.524953194 0.510431884 0.845615583 0.354514703 0.376046945 #> [43] 0.276615587 0.289749395 0.626388405 0.798022001 0.276332062 0.390676459 #> [49] 0.652266897 0.113525051 predict(fit, newdata = data.frame(time = c(1, 2, 5)),         type = "cumulative_hazard") #> [1] 0.2244716 0.5138491 1.5356737  # ── Patient-specific survival curves ───────────────────────────────── set.seed(1001) n   <- 180 dat <- data.frame(   time   = rexp(n, rate = 0.35) + 0.05,   status = rbinom(n, size = 1, prob = 0.6),   age    = rnorm(n, mean = 62, sd = 11),   nyha   = sample(1:4, n, replace = TRUE),   shock  = rbinom(n, size = 1, prob = 0.18) ) fit2 <- hazard(   survival::Surv(time, status) ~ age + nyha + shock,   data  = dat,   theta = c(mu = 0.25, nu = 1.10, beta1 = 0, beta2 = 0, beta3 = 0),   dist  = "weibull", fit = TRUE )  new_patients <- data.frame(   time = c(0.5, 1.5, 3.0),   age  = c(50, 65, 75),   nyha = c(1, 3, 4),   shock = c(0, 0, 1) ) # Compute predictions from the clean covariate frame before adding columns surv   <- predict(fit2, newdata = new_patients, type = "survival") cumhaz <- predict(fit2, newdata = new_patients, type = "cumulative_hazard") new_patients$survival          <- surv new_patients$cumulative_hazard <- cumhaz new_patients #>   time age nyha shock  survival cumulative_hazard #> 1  0.5  50    1     0 0.9494097        0.05191482 #> 2  1.5  65    3     0 0.7743185        0.25577202 #> 3  3.0  75    4     1 0.5046535        0.68388317  # \donttest{ # ── Grouped survival curves (requires hvtiPlotR) ───────────────────── if (requireNamespace("hvtiPlotR", quietly = TRUE) &&     requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE)) {   library(hvtiPlotR)    t_grid <- seq(0.05, max(dat$time), length.out = 80)   profiles <- data.frame(     label = c("Low risk (age 50, NYHA I)",               "High risk (age 75, NYHA IV)"),     age   = c(50, 75),     nyha  = c(1, 4),     shock = c(0, 1)   )    curve_list <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(profiles)), function(i) {     nd <- data.frame(       time  = t_grid,       age   = profiles$age[i],       nyha  = profiles$nyha[i],       shock = profiles$shock[i]     )     nd$survival <- predict(fit2, newdata = nd, type = "survival") * 100     nd$profile  <- profiles$label[i]     nd   })   curve_df <- do.call(rbind, curve_list)    hz_obj <- hv_hazard(     curve_data   = curve_df,     x_col        = "time",     estimate_col = "survival",     group_col    = "profile"   )    plot(hz_obj) +     ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100)) +     ggplot2::labs(       x     = "Years after surgery",       y     = "Freedom from death (%)",       title = "Predicted survival by risk profile"     ) +     hv_theme_manuscript() }  # }
